description: Free the specified ball velocities. As a result, ball motion reflects the forces acting upon them (velocities are updated during cycling according to equations of motion).

python_sdk_alternative.workaround: Ball.set_fix(component, False) where component: 0=X, 1=Y, 2=Z, 3=RX, 4=RY, 5=RZ - requires iteration

keywords.name: spin
keywords.description: The angular velocity is free.

keywords.name: spin-x
keywords.description: Free the \(x\) -component of the angular velocity.

keywords.name: spin-y
keywords.description: Free the \(y\) -component of the angular velocity.

keywords.name: spin-z
keywords.description: Free the \(z\) -component of the angular velocity.

keywords.name: velocity
keywords.description: The translational velocity is free.

keywords.name: velocity-x
keywords.description: Free the \(x\) -component of the translational velocity.

keywords.name: velocity-y
keywords.description: Free the \(y\) -component of the translational velocity.

keywords.name: velocity-z
keywords.description: Free the \(z\) -component of the translational velocity.

examples.description: Free all translational and angular velocity components for all balls
Free x-velocity for balls in 'left_balls' group
Free z-velocity for balls in y-position range (allow vertical motion)
Free all translational velocities for balls 1-100
